creator: mckusick@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Conrad Meyer from comment #24)
Thanks, doing the 'truncate -s 30g myvm.img' did the trick and I was able to then successfully growfs the filesystem. I did cause a panic because of having active snapshots, but that is a different bug that I know how to fix (and will do so shortly). So, bottom line is that I agree that it is entirely reasonable to make the images much smaller.
